text: Mass adoption of AI won't satisfice with a chat interface. @pmarca February was the best month in a][ history for consumer subscription app revenue. Yet most consumers still don't use AI. The reason is simple: chat is not the right UI for most people. We need AI-native interfaces that embed intelligence into workflows people already understand.
Everyone is talking about @cursor_ai, but if I were an investor, I'd prefer @windsurf_ai. Their engineering capabilities, especially in product design, far surpass those of Cursor.

I've always thought AI products need more than just a stronger LLM—great user experience demands innovative design in product and engineering. @windsurf_ai excels at this, and I've been curious about their unique choices. @kevinhou22's sharing gave me a glimpse into some of their thinking.
Been using @windsurf_ai for a while now. The attention to detail in their product design is remarkable — it's not just about the model, it's about how the entire experience is crafted.

Why every time I use Cursor, it makes me angry? It clearly hasn't submitted the code but claims it has, clearly hasn't read the files but says it did, always randomly updates unrelated original code... Every time I use Windsurf, it correctly identifies the problem and solves it.

How Generative AI will change the gaming industry?

A thread 🧵
Listening to @lexfridman's podcast on the drive. The depth of conversation and willingness to explore uncomfortable ideas is what makes it one of the best long-form content out there.
The best founders I know don't just build products — they build categories. Products can be copied. Categories shape how people think.
In the age of AI, the most underrated skill is knowing what questions to ask. The model gives you answers — but the quality of those answers is entirely determined by the quality of your questions.

We firmly believe that #generativeAI is not born to serve the traditional #games and #entertainment industry but to redefine how people create and interact with entertainment and fantasies.

They will certainly benefit greatly from it, but those are not the biggest scenarios.

Every great company starts with a personal obsession. The best products come from scratching your own itch — you understand the problem at a molecular level that no amount of user research can replicate.
